metric_value: Adaptive Computing — next-generation midrange/mainframe alternative via RMS clustering
notes: Aberdeen recommends abandoning commodity RISC/open-systems positioning for premium Adaptive Computing brand

metric_name: Worldwide financial and information services midrange/mainframe market size
notes: Aberdeen Group market sizing for combined financial and information services sectors

metric_name: Projected annual growth rate for target market
metric_value: 19% per annum
notes: Projects market reaching ~$20B by 1994; above industry average growth rate

metric_value: Reliability
notes: Seven key buying reasons in priority order; reliability is paramount for financial transactions

metric_value: Availability (24x7 uptime)
notes: Around-the-clock operations required for multinational financial markets

metric_value: Expandability
notes: New applications grow at 30% per year post-installation

metric_value: Performance (throughput)
notes: Financial industry generates highest transaction volumes of all industries

metric_value: Investment protection
notes: Open systems with C/Unix protects software investment; VME bus protects hardware investment

metric_value: Applications availability
notes: Lack of Unix-based enterprise applications is high barrier to entry for non-IBM platforms

metric_value: Price
notes: Price is lowest priority; financial markets pay premium for mission-critical reliability

metric_value: Rifle-shot niche strategy via Advanced Technology Planning organizations
notes: Financial market too broad for general campaign; target ATP groups at large financial firms

metric_value: SMP + Clustering + Memory-Coupling + Single-System View + High Availability
notes: Only architecture combining all five attributes simultaneously; synergy is the value proposition

metric_value: 1000-2000 transactions per second
notes: Aberdeen recommends Encore demonstrate competitive TPC-A benchmark to validate Adaptive Computing claims

metric_value: Two dual-processor Series 91s + 16 MB RMS + 4 GB DASD = ~$250,000
notes: Minimum financial commitment for serious trial in financial/information services data center

metric_value: Adaptive 893: 8x Series 93 + 1 GB RAM + 4-64MB RMS + 12 GB DASD = ~$5 million
notes: High-end configuration; price/MIPS should increase with scale to protect margins

metric_name: IBM S/390 competitive position in financial services
metric_value: Market leader with strong grip despite high costs and DB2 performance limitations
notes: Users displeased with DB2 performance above 100 users; still dominant due to entrenchment

metric_value: ~50% of product revenues from financial sector
notes: Well-established with excellent references; fault tolerance prized for certain applications

metric_value: Migrating from proprietary to MIPS Risc/Unix; transition incomplete during planning period
notes: Aberdeen predicts transition will absorb engineering resources through 1993

metric_value: 20000 VAXcluster installations
notes: Primarily composed of low-performance VAXes; distributed lock manager a known weakness

metric_value: $1M-$1.2M initial + $0.5M upgrades over 18 months
notes: Configuration pattern documented; originally deployed at Citicorp and Bank of America

metric_value: $100K-$150K primary sales range
notes: Lacks robust cluster strategy; Encore can win head-to-head with RMS-based clusters

metric_value: Can displace IBM S/390 in RDBMS applications with superior price/performance
notes: Bold prediction contingent on TPC-A benchmarks and direct sales investment

metric_name: Encore financial services market penetration — actual outcome
metric_value: Failed to penetrate financial services; ceased operations 1999
notes: Aberdeen's optimistic scenario never materialized; financial difficulties prevented market investment

metric_value: Can establish new architectural standard in financial/information services through 1990s
notes: Contingent on significant sales force investment and benchmark achievements

metric_name: Encore actual commercial outcome — storage division sale
metric_value: Sold storage business to Sun Microsystems for $185M in July 1997 to retire JEC debt
notes: Primary proceeds went to Japan Energy Corporation (JEC) debt repayment; shareholders received ~$0.025/share

metric_name: Encore actual outcome — real-time division sale
metric_value: Sold real-time business to Gores Technology Group for $3M in January 1999; operations ceased
notes: Became Encore Real-Time Computing under Gores; later acquired by Compro Computer Services 2002

metric_value: IBM mainframe evolved to zSeries (2000) then IBM z Systems; still dominant in financial services
notes: Aberdeen's assessment of IBM's strong grip on financial markets proved correct

metric_value: Acquired by Compaq for ~$3 billion in 1997; Compaq acquired by HP 2002; HP NonStop still active
notes: NonStop fault-tolerant platform survives as Hewlett Packard Enterprise NonStop division

metric_value: Acquired by Compaq for $9.6B in January 1998; Compaq acquired by HP 2002; VAXcluster discontinued
notes: Aberdeen correctly predicted DEC was phasing out VAX; transition to Alpha ultimately unsuccessful

metric_value: Architecture has staying power to last through seven-year buyer lifecycle (rest of 1990s)
notes: Based on SMP + clustering + memory coupling synergy being unique in 1991

metric_name: Financial market transaction volume — retail banking (S&L)
metric_value: 25 large S&Ls requiring >50 TPS
notes: Retail banking TPS requirements documented; S&L industry noted to have widespread problems

metric_name: Financial market transaction volume — credit/debit card processing
metric_value: 100-300 TPS (regional to central switch)
notes: Requires multi-million dollar mainframes with fault-tolerant front ends; high lucrative transaction fees

metric_name: Financial market transaction volume — retail brokerage
metric_value: 75 TPS peak at large brokerage firm
notes: Multiple computers managing flow from broker desktop through wire rooms to exchange floor

metric_name: Financial market transaction volume — mutual funds
metric_value: 100 TPS shareholder + 150 TPS opening quotes
notes: Stock ticker plant application on fault-tolerant computer; minimal database activity for quotes

metric_value: Highly fragmented with no single direct marketing vehicle (no unifying trade shows or publications)
notes: Large players ADP / Compuserve / Dow Jones identified as prospects despite fragmentation
